description: A referendum held on October 30, 1995, in the Canadian province of Quebec, asking voters if Quebec should proclaim sovereignty and become an independent country. The result was a narrow victory for the 'No' side, with 50.58% against independence and 49.42% in favor.

key_events.description: Jacques Parizeau formally launches the sovereignty campaign.
The 'Oui' side gains momentum following a televised debate.
The 'Love-in' rally in Montreal galvanizes the 'No' side.
Final vote count shows the narrowest margin in Canadian history.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling extreme polarization and late-stage momentum shifts in a high-stakes constitutional referendum.
